title: Energy Tracking with Variables
description: Use variables to track and manage your energy levels dynamically across farming operations.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Now that you know about the energy system, let's use variables to track it dynamically!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: Remember get_energy()? It returns a dictionary with 'energy' and 'maxEnergy'. We can store these!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Try this: energy_data = get_energy(), then access the current energy with energy_data['energy']

objectives.description: Store energy data in a variable
Access the energy value from the dictionary

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Perfect! Now you can check your energy programmatically and make decisions based on it.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Let's calculate if you have enough energy for a farming route. Movement costs 5 per tile, plant and harvest cost energy too.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: Store your current energy, calculate the cost, then check if you can afford it!

objectives.description: Calculate energy cost for operations via 'energy_cost' variable
Compare current energy to cost using 'current_energy' variable

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Excellent budgeting! You're thinking like a programmer - plan before executing.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Here's an advanced technique: store your starting energy, perform operations, then calculate how much you used!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: This helps optimize your code - you can see which operations are most expensive.

objectives.description: Store starting energy before operations via 'start_energy' variable

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Brilliant! You're tracking energy consumption. This is essential for optimizing large farming operations.

phases.title: Dynamic Energy Decisions
phases.description: Use energy tracking for smart decisions

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Final challenge: farm multiple plots, but stop and print a warning if your energy drops below 30.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: Use variables to track energy after each operation. Print a message when it gets low!

objectives.description: Use print() to warn about low energy

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Perfect! You're using variables to make your code smarter and more adaptive.

postDialogues.name: Qubit
postDialogues.content: Next, we'll learn conditionals - then you can automatically eat when energy is low!
